findings.evidence: `rg` shows the file itself describes this theorem as “non-inversions plus inversions” at lines 101-104, while Mathlib’s `CoxeterSystem.IsLeftDescent`/`IsRightDescent` names descents for simple-generator length drops (`Mathlib/GroupTheory/Coxeter/Length.lean:265-268`) and uses separate `IsLeftInversion`/`IsRightInversion` terminology for inversions (`Mathlib/GroupTheory/Coxeter/Inversion.lean:125-131`).
findings.fix: Rename to use the terminology of the statement and docstring, e.g. `card_filter_noninversion_add_card_filter_inversion` or `card_filter_lt_add_card_filter_gt`.
findings.issue: `card_filter_ascent_add_card_filter_descent` names the two filters as ascents/descents, but the statement counts arbitrary pairs `p.1 < p.2` with `x p.1 < x p.2` or `x p.2 < x p.1`; these are non-inversions/inversions, not descents in the usual adjacent/simple-generator sense.

summary: One introduced theorem name uses nonstandard and internally inconsistent terminology for arbitrary permutation pairs.
